message:
ffs-core + ffs-journal: aggregate statfs free counts from GDs; JBD2 tag format overhaul

Two coupled changes that land together because the ffs-core test
suite exercises behaviour from both layers.

### ffs-core statfs: aggregate from group descriptors

`OpenFs::statfs` on the ext4 branch previously sourced `blocks_free`
and `files_free` from the top-level superblock counters
(`sb.free_blocks_count`, `sb.free_inodes_count`). Those counters
are only refreshed on sync / unmount on real ext4, so they drift
from live allocator state across mutations within a mount. On
frankenfs that meant a test that wrote 8 KiB and then called
statfs would see unchanged `blocks_free` / `files_free`, even though
the allocator had definitely consumed blocks and inodes.

Rewrite the ext4 branch of statfs to:

- Compute geometry via `FsGeometry::from_superblock(sb)` to get
  `group_count`.
- Iterate every group descriptor with
  `self.read_group_desc_with_scope(cx, scope, GroupNumber(idx))`
  under the live `cx`/`scope` so the read participates in the
  request's snapshot policy.
- Accumulate `free_blocks_count` and `free_inodes_count` with
  `saturating_add` on `u64` to guard against corrupt descriptors
  summing past `u64::MAX`.
- Clamp the aggregated totals to the superblock-declared totals
  (`.min(sb.blocks_count)`, `.min(u64::from(sb.inodes_count))`) so
  a descriptor overflow still produces physically meaningful
  numbers that pass the downstream invariant
  `blocks_free <= blocks` / `files_free <= files`.

The pre-existing `blocks_available = blocks_free - reserved_blocks_count`
subtraction is retained; it still uses `saturating_sub` so reserved
> free (which on a fresh image with reserved_blocks_count > 0 is
possible) degrades to 0 rather than underflowing.

Signature change: the method now needs `cx` / `scope` non-ignored
(was `_cx`, `_scope`) because it issues per-group reads. No public
API break — they were already parameters.

Re-export addition: `ReleaseRequest` added to the public vfs re-export
block so the evidence ledger commit in a sibling change can refer to
it without duplicating the import path.

Test additions:

- `open_fs_fsops_statfs_ext4_aggregates_group_descriptor_counts`
  replaces `open_fs_fsops_statfs_ext4_uses_superblock_counts`:
    * Builds the usual ext4 image with dir.
    * Writes 3 / 63 / 127 into the superblock blocks_count,
      free_blocks_count, free_inodes_count *slots* — but
    * Uses a new helper `set_group_desc_free_counts(image, 42, 17)`
      to stuff 42 / 17 into the actual group descriptor fields.
    * Asserts statfs reports `blocks_free == 42`,
      `blocks_available == 39` (= 42 − reserved_blocks_count 3),
      `files_free == 17`. If the statfs path were still reading
      the superblock fields it would report 63 / 60 / 127.
- `open_fs_good_internal_journal_checksum_replays_by_default`
  (journal side): build an ext4 image with a good internal JBD2
  journal checksum, open with default options, assert the replay
  path finds exactly commit sequence `[1]`, replays 1 block, and
  that block 15 ends up containing `b"JBD2-CSUM-REPLAY"` — which
  only matches if the CSUM_V3 tag checksum is computed correctly
  (see ffs-journal section below).
- Mutation-reflecting statfs regression: the existing
  `open_fs_fsops_statfs_post_mutation` test previously allowed
  `blocks_free` / `files_free` to stay unchanged after a write
  (with a comment explaining ext4 reads the superblock snapshot).
  That comment now describes the pre-fix behaviour; the test now
  snapshots `before = fs.statfs(...)` up-front and asserts
  `after.blocks_free < before.blocks_free` and
  `after.files_free < before.files_free` with diagnostic messages
  that include both numbers. This is a positive regression guard
  against the statfs path ever reverting to superblock reads.

### ffs-journal: CSUM_V2 / CSUM_V3 tag layouts + per-tag checksums

The JBD2 descriptor-tag parser only handled the legacy 8/12-byte
tag shape. Real Linux JBD2 journals shipped with
`feature_incompat.JBD2_FEATURE_INCOMPAT_CSUM_V2` add a 2-byte
`t_checksum` (10/14-byte tags); `CSUM_V3` switches to a 16-byte
base tag with a 32-bit checksum at a different offset. Missing
that meant replay skipped journals silently or mis-applied
descriptors for modern mkfs outputs.

Full layout constants added next to the legacy ones so offsets
are spelled out:

```
JBD2_TAG_SIZE_CSUM_V2_32      = 10
JBD2_TAG_SIZE_CSUM_V2_64      = 14
JBD2_TAG_SIZE_CSUM_V3         = 16
JBD2_TAG_UUID_SIZE            = 16
JBD2_TAG_FLAGS_OFFSET_V1_V2   =  6
JBD2_TAG_CHECKSUM_OFFSET_V1_V2 = 4
JBD2_TAG_FLAGS_OFFSET_V3      =  4
JBD2_TAG_HIGH_OFFSET_V3       =  8
JBD2_TAG_CHECKSUM_OFFSET_V3   = 12
```

`Jbd2Superblock::tag_format()` (new private helper) centralises
the "which tag layout does this journal use?" decision as a
`Jbd2TagFormat { Legacy, CsumV2, CsumV3 }` enum based on the
incompat flags. Every descriptor-parsing site now consults it
once rather than re-doing the feature bitmask.

`Jbd2Superblock::csum_seed` extended: CSUM_V2 now uses the same
`!crc32c(!0, uuid)` seed that CSUM_V3 already used. Pre-change it
returned 0 for CSUM_V2, which is only correct for the legacy
mode. The 0-seed path is now reached only for genuinely legacy
journals (neither CSUM_V2 nor CSUM_V3 set).

`struct DescriptorTag` gains `data_checksum: Option<u32>`. The
parser populates it for CSUM_V2 (16-bit, zero-extended into u32)
and CSUM_V3 (32-bit). Legacy tags get `None`. Replay code then
knows when to verify per-block checksums vs. when to trust the
descriptor's own end-block checksum only.

The actual tag-walking loop in the descriptor parser is restructured
so the tag stride advances by the format-specific constant above,
and the SAME_UUID / !SAME_UUID trailing-UUID carve-out runs at the
right offset for each format (16 bytes past the base tag in CSUM_V3
vs past-the-checksum in V2/legacy). Bad tag lengths (descriptor
runs out of block mid-tag) now cleanly abort the replay rather
than panicking.

Helpers added to the ffs-core test module to drive the new path:

- `checksum_jbd2_data_block_for_test(block, sequence, seed)` —
  computes the expected per-block crc32c that CSUM_V2 / CSUM_V3
  tags should carry (matches the JBD2 reference implementation's
  sequence-then-block chained crc).
- `stamp_test_jbd2_csum3_tag_checksum(descriptor, tag_offset,
  data_block, sequence, seed)` — stamps that checksum into the
  CSUM_V3 tag's `t_checksum` field (4-byte, big-endian, offset 12
  inside the tag).

These are used by `open_fs_good_internal_journal_checksum_replays_
by_default` above, and by an updated flow in the existing
`open_fs_nojournal_checksum_replays_internal_journal_with_bad_checksum`
helper chain that now stamps the CSUM_V3 tag before the descriptor
checksum (was omitted before the rework).

No public API break in ffs-journal; the old `csum_seed()` and
`DescriptorTag` remain publicly reachable and now just carry
correct values across the v2/v3 feature matrix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
